item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for issues given two posts above by Person243

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I mean… it's okay. Yet another example of "black highlighter doesn't make an SCP good". It's average, nothing more nothing less. Not to mention, it never tells us how *wide* the damn shrimp is, meaning it's difficult to really visualize it. -1, nothing personal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a couple of problems with this. First, a two light-year long object rotating in a few minutes would be moving much faster than light. But maybe that's an anomalous property it has.
Also, at its size and distance I think it would be about 16 degrees across in the sky, while the moon is only 1/2 a degree. Otherwise good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is excellent, +1

People here are very concerned about the mass and general ridiculousness: consider that the shrimp may very well (and in my opinion is) some form of hologram.

The mass is never observed by the foundation, they claim
"The gravitational pull alone would dismantle our solar system entirely"
but they do not say that they have observed any pull, it could be mere speculation based on the estimated volume.

Also, I would love to see a little more investigation, I find it hard to believe that the Foundation would stop at this:

"The billboard was rented by Brant Seymour (POI-5909-A), manager of Lockhart's Red Lobster restaurant. Red Lobster Corporate as well as their parent-company Darden have refused to answer any questions"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is Shrimp Heaven.

Always a fan of anomalous advertising, served steaming hot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They had us in the first half, not gonna lie

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I want red lobster, It worked. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Imagine a 2m tall person standing 8m away from you: this shrimp would take up about as much of your field of view.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
amazingly dumb
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve decided that the outrageously impossible science of this dumb thing (as demonstrated throughout this thread) just makes me like it even more. +1

item.title: Re: Gravitational Effects of the Prawn
item.content:encoded: Water at room temperature has a density of 1000kg/m3, so 1.5*1053kg of matter would have a volume of 1.5*1050m3, or about 177 cubic light years.

item.title: Re: Gravitational Effects of the Prawn
item.content:encoded: If all the mass in the universe was gathered together with the density of water (roughly), how big would it be? I won't even try to estimate with my silly human brain, but a cubic light-year is big, so it's not unreasonable.

item.title: Re: Gravitational Effects of the Prawn
item.content:encoded: Note that the mass of the observable universe is 1.5 × 1053 kg, or 1/1.2Mth your estimated mass for the shrimp.

item.title: Gravitational Effects of the Prawn
item.content:encoded:
Really liked the article.
It mentions that gravitational issues would be destructive if it comes within "even 1 light year" of the Sol System. So I took the liberty of calculating it's actual gravitational effects.

Assumptions: The shrimp is a scaled up version of an average shrimp. Base length and mass assumed to be 6cm and 50g. If it is scaled up linearly in length, there is a cubed relationship between the initial and final masses. That is, if you double the length (and all other dimensions) of something, you octuple (23 = 8) its mass.

Then we use F=Gm1m2/r2

So with that we have:
Ratio of lengths = 2.1ly / 6cm = 3.4*1023
Ratio of masses = (3.4*1023)3 = 3.6*1070
Mass of giant shrimp (m1) = 0.05kg * 3.6*1070 = 1.8*1069 kg
Mass of Earth (m2)= 5.9*1024 kg
Distance between the two masses (r) = 7.5ly = 7.1*1016m
Gravitational Constant (G) = 6.7*10-11

Gravitational Force = 6.7*10-11 * 1.8*1069 * 5.9*1024/(7.1*1016)2 = 1.4*1050 N
Earth's acceleration towards shrimp = 2.4*1025 m/s2

In other words, we would have reached and smacked into the Shrimp after 0.000077 seconds at a speed (not accounting for relativity, instead ) that would be greater than the speed of light by a very wide margin (7.5 years after the Shrimp's appearance due to the travel time of gravitational waves at the speed of light).

In other words, the appearance of this shrimp would probably mean the end of the universe, simply through its existence.

Of course, this shrimp is anomalous, so all my assumptions are likely wrong, but I thought it would be fun to check.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ridiculous… I love it

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Say hello to SCP-2154.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah the lack of anything addressing the naked eye visibility of a giant space shrimp was a significant factor in my downvote as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an understandable view, I meant this to be a quick little doodad and knew before posting it wouldn't.be for everyone. I appreciate you reading and leaving a comment nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a valid point, but in the proposal it states they will need several anomalies to transport the bomb. I'd assume that to include some sort of remote viewing anomaly in order to accurately pinpoint the shrimp's real-time location.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The plot problem I see here is that if it was located nine light years from earth, we would be observing its behaviour on a nine-year delay. How would the Foundation/GoC know where to transport the bomb if they're going on decade-old data?

item.title: Re: The Endless Shrimp
item.content:encoded: Tasty +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hesitant to vote either way. On one hand, it's a strange object that is certainly unique and got a chuckle out of me, but on the other hand, I feel like there could have been more. The entire article felt like it was going by very fast, sorta like a NASCAR race. It's cool and an interesting thing, but it's fast and hard to see the interesting moments happening because it's over soon after it begins. It deserves to remain on the site, but I don't feel comfortable giving it a downvote nor an upvote.

item.title: Since Nobody even mentions that an object so massive would collapse upon itself, due to it's own gravity, I am.
(no title)
item.content:encoded: I've got some bad news for you, friend…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who wouldn't want to be disintegrated by a shellfish moving at astronomical speeds. All that i'm saying is if i'm going to die, this is how I would want to do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I was about to say. At that size, at that distance, it would take up a huge portion of the sky. There would be religions built around it, Copernicus would have theorized that it was the object around which the earth orbited, a small but vocal group of people would spend their lives trying to prove that it was flat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh with those numbers it would appear 16 times as far across in the sky as the moon. (The moon is roughly 1 degree across, and at 7.5 light years distance this thing would be about 16 degrees.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a couple of technical faults I see here (writing technique not tech team)

During your last log, you say"it releases" a brown substance which doesn't describe what "it" is.

There's a big setup here with only half the payoff imo. The lack of any foundation reaction, plus the unclear details about what exactly is happening makes me -1 here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That plot twist… the drama… the pacing… perfection. Congrats for being the first article I comment on. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why i laughin so hard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: classic Aki. ffs, take your +1 please.

item.title: Reality erasing bomb on a light year sized shrimp shaped restaurant ad
item.content:encoded: +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awful +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…god fucking damn it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I both hate and love this. I was mostly expecting some sort of thing with the Foundation having to cover up the fact that they erased part of reality, then suddenly it's just a space advert for a regular Earth restaurant.

+1, you've earned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm, yes. I'll take one space shrimp please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As brief as this is, I have to say it hits that nice "WTF" kind of atmosphere that makes the whole experience fairly enjoyable to me as a reader. While there are a few areas in here you could expand on (ie, what SCPs would be used in the neutralization effort, etc), its probably better to err on the side of brevity than risk overstaying your welcome.

Also, the Red Lobster bit got a legitimate laugh out of me, IRL, so hats off to you there.

+1, well done DrAkimoto.

item.title: The Endless Shrimp
item.content:encoded:
I'm just gonna blame XilasCrowe for encouraging me to write this.

Images: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_sky_full_of_stars_NGC_4833.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Template:Potd/2006-10-10_(pl)

GOC + Foundation logo was created by Calibri Bold

Crit credit: Jacob Conwell, cybersqyd, Dr Moned, OCuin, spicaterrible, Oboebandgeek99, Calibri Bold
